rulings.Case Title: THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON VS. ALBERT H WONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 9, 2020 line 1. DEFENDANTS ALBERT WONG, JOYCE WONG'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to amend the complaint in accordance with its supplemental brief sections II and IV. The Court does not reach the issue of the Statute of Limitations at this time. Such issue may be raised if necessary and proper upon the filing of the amended complaint. Plaintiff shall have 30 days from notice of entry of order to file its amended complaint. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JULIANA CLEGG VS. WILMINGTON TRUST, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disregard And Set Aside And Referees Tentative Report And Recommendations Re Plaintiffs Motion For Sanctions And Objections Thereto
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 9, 2020 line 5. DEFENDANTS FAY SERVICING, LLC, WILMINGTON TRUST'S, Motion To Disregard And Set Aside And Referees Tentative Report And Recommendations Re Plaintiffs Motion For Sanctions And Objections Thereto Hearing Required. Hearing Required. By no later than 8:30 a.m. on November 9, 2020 Defendant to file (a hard courtesy copy to be delivered to department 501) a declaration stating that: (1) appropriate city officials have been contacted and an inspection re: repairs has been scheduled; and (2) all appropriate permits have been obtained or, if no permits have been obtained, state the authority allowing for repairs without permits. Declarations shall be provided by individuals competent to testify on these matters having personal knowledge. Declaration shall not exceed 1 page and shall be limited to the issues listed above. By no later than 8:30 a.m. moving party shall provide the following supplemental brief (a hard courtesy copy to be delivered to department 501) in a table format. The brief shall include all testimony provided by Ms. Sequete appearing for "Wilmington Trust, National Association" be it on July 1 or July 2, 2020. (NOTE: Moving papers included only one transcript of Sequete Deposition, Ex. J Wednesday, July 1, 2020, including pages 1-57); if supplemental brief refers to any other transcripts, copies of such transcripts shall also be provided). The brief shall include all question asked at the Wilmington PMQ deposition and shall be in the following format: Column One: State the Question asked by Plaintiff's Counsel with citation to the transcript. (e.g. "Has Wilmington ever given Fay, reimbursed Fay for any of the money they paid on attorney's fees Ex. J 11:4-6). Column Two: Identify if the question was pursuant to any of the Topics covered in the Notice of Deposition for Wilmington Trust PMQ. If question was asked on one of the designated topics, state that number of and quote the topic. (e.g. Topic 1. The amount that you have disbursed on the LOAN for attorney's fees and litigation costs for Clegg I). If Defendant asserts that the question was outside of the Notice of Deposition topics, state "none." Column Three: Quote the response provided to the question. If response references previously provided responses, quote the previous response. Provide citations to all quoted portions of the transcripts. (E.g. "I don't know." Ex J 11:7; "It would be the same amount that I was testified to yesterday that Fay disbursed in the loan." Ex. J 10:7-8 AND quote and cite to the testimony provided "yesterday."). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID TSAO VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Move For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 9, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Move For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions This appears to be a motion to re-open discovery in order for Defendant to file a motion for terminating sanctions. Defendant is not seeking terminating sanctions by this motion. Plaintiff initially filed this unlawful detainer summary action, expecting Defendant to respond to a 5-day summons. However, Plaintiff appears to not have been prepared to respond to the most basic discovery on the 5-day turn-around and appears to have been caught by surprise and unable to provide complete responses to the Form Interrogatories and Request for Production regarding the documents forming the prima facie case, i.e. the existence of the landlord-tenant relationship, the service of the operative notice, proof of breach asserted in the notice, etc. Instead such evidence was allegedly discovered in March and September 2020, i.e. 6 and 16 months after this summary proceeding was initiated. Just as Plaintiff was apparently not prepared to efficiently proceed wit this Unlawful Detainer summary proceeding in August 2019, Plaintiff failed to efficiently proceed with this action when possession was surrendered. Parties agree that possession was surrendered before September 28, 2020. As of November 6, 2020 Plaintiff has not taken any steps to either dismiss this action or covert it to a general civil action (e.g. pay the additional filing fee and proceed on the existing complaint or file a motion for leave to file an amended complaint). This lack of action on Plaintiff's behalf left this action in a procedural limbo: this is no longer an unlawful detainer and it is not yet a general civil action. Defendant's motion, therefore, is either premature or moot. It Plaintiff is not proceeding with this action as is, the motion is moot. If Plaintiff converts this action into a general civil action, the discovery will automatically be re-opened and will proceed in accordance with civil action procedures. Defendant's motion is taken off calendar. By no later than 9:00 a.m. on November 9, 2020 Plaintiff to file a declaration (a hard courtesy copy to be delivered to department 501) detailing its next steps in proceeding with this action: the filing of a dismissal; conversion of the action by appropriate means, etc. including specific dates for such actions. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DELAWARE LAKEWOOD APARTMENTS, LLC. VS. SAHAQ HOVSEPIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 9, 2020 line 3. DEFENDANT'S SHAQ HOVSEPIAN MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Hearing Required. Mr. Hovsepian and Ms. Egger to be present to testify as necessary. Appearance may be in-person or via Zoom. Zoom appearance is encouraged. The Court will provide a Zoom meeting link. To obtain the link, counsel for both sides or unrepresented parties to e-mail Department501ContestTR@sftc.org no later than 5:00 p.m. on October 30, 2020. E-mail subject line must include the case name; the case number; and the hearing date. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ERIC GALLANTER VS. MICHAEL SIZEMORE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 9, 2020 line 4. DEFENDANT'S MICHAEL SIZEMORE'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts re: landlord-tenant relationship with the moving defendant. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that a mere allegation that the Defendant is a subsequent occupant factually establishes a landlord-tenant relationship. E.g. See definition of tenant SFRO 37.2(t). = (501/CFH)
